impact: basejump.trigger_set_timestamps() (defined at supabase/migrations/20240414161707_basejump-setup.sql) uses now() which returns transaction-start time. Within a single BEGIN/ROLLBACK pgTAP test, INSERT then UPDATE both produce the same timestamp → cmp_ok(updated_at > created_at) always fails. Affects T-004's test (21-dim-persoon-en-functie.sql line 90) which has the same broken assertion. Fixes: (a) modify trigger to use clock_timestamp() — invasive; (b) use two-transaction test pattern — pgTAP doesn't natively support; (c) drop the trigger test entirely and rely on visual code review of the trigger definition. Option (c) is the POC-pragmatic call; T-007 test already omits this assertion.
resolution:
T-004 test 21-dim-persoon-en-functie.sql updated: broken cmp_ok(updated_at > created_at) assertion removed, plan(16) → plan(15), inline documentation notes the transaction-time limitation of basejump.trigger_set_timestamps and points back to ISS-012. T-007 test already omits the assertion. Trigger correctness itself verified by inspection; not a runtime bug, only a test-authoring pitfall. Upstream fix (clock_timestamp() in basejump migration) is out of scope for POC — would require a Basejump patch.</resolution>
</invoke>

title: basejump.trigger_set_timestamps uses now(), broken for same-tx updated_at tests
